mnemonics: Latches Latch onto Levels: They are level-sensitive and their output depends on the continuous value of the enable input.
keyPoints: A latch is a basic bistable memory element that stores one bit of data.
Latches are level-sensitive (transparent) devices; when the enable signal is active, the output follows the input.
The most basic types are the SR Latch (Set-Reset) and the D Latch (Data).
commonMistakes: Confusing latches with flip-flops; latches are level-triggered, while flip-flops are edge-triggered.
Forgetting that a latch's output can change multiple times while its enable signal is active, leading to potential timing issues in synchronous circuits.
